name: Railway Platform API
what_it_does: Developer-friendly cloud platform with GraphQL API — deploying any language/framework from GitHub with built-in databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis), networking, and volume storage, with transparent usage-based pricing and GraphQL API for infrastructure management.
use_cases: Deploying services from GitHub repositories programmatically via Railway GraphQL API
Provisioning managed databases (PostgreSQL, Redis, MySQL) via API
Querying deployment logs and service metrics for operational visibility
Managing environment variables and service configuration programmatically
Creating project templates and deploying from Infrastructure as Code
not_for: Enterprise compliance requirements (Railway lacks SOC2; use AWS or Render)
Stateful workloads beyond standard managed databases
High-traffic production services requiring multi-region deployment
best_when: A developer or small team wants the simplest possible deployment experience with built-in databases, transparent pricing, and GraphQL API for infrastructure automation.
avoid_when: You need SOC2/enterprise compliance, multi-region deployment, complex VPC networking, or very high-scale production workloads.

auth.notes: API token from Railway account settings. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. GraphQL API (not REST). No OAuth2 or scope granularity. Webhook for deployment events.

pricing.paid_tiers: Pro from $20/month; Team from $20/seat/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Generous free tier with monthly credit. Usage-based pricing (CPU, RAM, bandwidth, disk) — transparent and predictable. US-based San Francisco company. Founded 2020. YC-backed. Popular with indie developers and startups. GraphQL API is the primary programmatic interface.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL API for projects, services, deployments, databases, and volumes. Bearer token auth. Webhook for deploy events. Good documentation for GraphQL schema. GraphQL-only (no REST) — requires GraphQL client. Transparent usage-based pricing enables cost prediction.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Service creation idempotent by name. Deployment triggering creates new deploys. Database provisioning idempotent by service. Configuration updates idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL-only API — must use GraphQL client, not simple HTTP client
No SOC2 certification — not suitable for enterprise compliance requirements
Usage-based pricing can surprise — monitor CPU/RAM usage carefully
GitHub integration required for most deployment workflows

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. No formal security certifications (SOC2 not obtained). US data residency. Bearer token auth — no scope granularity. Environment variable encryption. Service isolation between projects. Standard startup security practices.
